data <- read.csv("CHS_cohortE(in).csv")
head(data)
##      id townabbr  age male               race         hisp asthma height weight
## 1 54577       SA 15.1    1             Others     Hispanic      0    168   52.0
## 2 50863       SD 16.5    1              Mixed     Hispanic     NA    168   50.2
## 3 52081       SD 15.6    0          Caucasian Non-Hispanic      0    167   55.6
## 4 53817       RV 15.2    1 Unknown or Missing     Hispanic      0    160   60.9
## 5 54683       GL 14.2    1          Caucasian Non-Hispanic      1    169   62.1
## 6 55339       SA 15.2    0          Caucasian Non-Hispanic      0    161   53.3
##    bmi educ          HomeBuilt BaseGasstove BasePets ETS_base wheeze fev1  fvc
## 1 18.4    1      1980 or later            1        1        0      0 4090 4950
## 2 17.8    1 Unknown or Missing            0        0        0     NA 3790 4810
## 3 19.9    3     1960s to 1970s            1        1        0      0 3240 3370
## 4 23.8    2 Unknown or Missing            1        0        0      0 3890 4190
## 5 21.8    5        Before 1960            1        1        0      0 3730 4930
## 6 20.6    5        Before 1960            0        1        0      0 3530 4010
##        pm25   sulfate  nitrate        ec      dust longitude latitude
## 1  8.839583 0.9295338 1.866360 0.7023304 0.4493519 -119.7511 34.50721
## 2 14.283841 1.3793629 2.280034 0.8730637 1.3024283 -117.8198 34.11693
## 3 15.000303 1.4643225 2.481278 0.8835121 1.2464851 -117.7939 34.06783
## 4 15.759308 1.5685237 2.451100 0.7618690 1.2896408 -117.3499 34.00193
## 5 14.184785 1.3247208 2.179399 0.8929864 1.3396275 -117.8885 34.14714
## 6  8.700749 0.9910168 1.774088 0.6733717 0.5512667 -119.6944 34.59196

Quantitative data charts

install.packages(“ggplot2”)

## There are cleaner plots with 'ggplot2'
library(ggplot2)

numeric_data <- data[sapply(data, is.numeric)]

for (col in names(numeric_data)) {
print(
  ggplot(data, aes_string(x = col)) + 
  geom_histogram(fill = "blue", bins = 30) + 
  ggtitle(paste("Histogram of", col))
)
}
## Warning: `aes_string()` was deprecated in ggplot2 3.0.0.
## ℹ Please use tidy evaluation idioms with `aes()`.
## ℹ See also `vignette("ggplot2-in-packages")` for more information.
## This warning is displayed once per session.
## Call `lifecycle::last_lifecycle_warnings()` to see where this warning was
## generated.

## (`stat_bin()`).

## (`stat_bin()`).

## (`stat_bin()`).

## (`stat_bin()`).

## check length
length(data$male)
## [1] 1000
length(data$stove)
## [1] 0
length(data$pets)
## [1] 0
## get 2x2 tables to check dependence of variable
table(data$male, data$asthma)
##    
##       0   1
##   0 418  99
##   1 369 110
vars <- c("male", "asthma")

for (v in vars) {
  print(v)
  print(table(data$asthma, data[[v]]))
}
## [1] "male"
##    
##       0   1
##   0 418 369
##   1  99 110
## [1] "asthma"
##    
##       0   1
##   0 787   0
##   1   0 209
vars <- c("male", "stove", "pets")
## getting only TRUE quantitative variables

quant_vars <- names(data)[sapply(data, function(x) {
  is.numeric(x) && !all(na.omit(unique(x)) %in% c(0,1))
})]

quant_vars
##  [1] "id"        "age"       "height"    "weight"    "bmi"       "educ"     
##  [7] "fev1"      "fvc"       "pm25"      "sulfate"   "nitrate"   "ec"       
## [13] "dust"      "longitude" "latitude"
prop.table(table(data$asthma, data$male))
##    
##              0          1
##   0 0.41967871 0.37048193
##   1 0.09939759 0.11044177
## plot quantitative variables

for (v in quant_vars) {
  hist(data[[v]],
       main = paste("Histogram of", v),
       xlab = v)
}

for (v in quant_vars) {
  boxplot(data[[v]] ~ data$asthma,
          main = paste(v, "vs Asthma"),
          xlab = "Asthma",
          ylab = v)
}

## comparing numeric vs groups usint t test

for (v in quant_vars) {
  cat("\n====================\n")
  cat("Variable:", v, "\n")
  
  temp <- na.omit(data[, c(v, "asthma")])
  print(t.test(temp[[v]] ~ temp$asthma))
}
## 
## ====================
## Variable: id 
## 
##  Welch Two Sample t-test
## 
## data:  temp[[v]] by temp$asthma
## t = 0.25586, df = 333.78, p-value = 0.7982
## alternative hypothesis: true difference in means between group 0 and group 1 is not equal to 0
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  -233.6297  303.4922
## sample estimates:
## mean in group 0 mean in group 1 
##        53010.42        52975.49 
## 
## 
## ====================
## Variable: age 
## 
##  Welch Two Sample t-test
## 
## data:  temp[[v]] by temp$asthma
## t = 1.082, df = 332.99, p-value = 0.28
## alternative hypothesis: true difference in means between group 0 and group 1 is not equal to 0
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  -0.04143404  0.14273691
## sample estimates:
## mean in group 0 mean in group 1 
##        15.26213        15.21148 
## 
## 
## ====================
## Variable: height 
## 
##  Welch Two Sample t-test
## 
## data:  temp[[v]] by temp$asthma
## t = -1.3591, df = 338, p-value = 0.175
## alternative hypothesis: true difference in means between group 0 and group 1 is not equal to 0
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  -2.1710281  0.3968022
## sample estimates:
## mean in group 0 mean in group 1 
##        166.0076        166.8947 
## 
## 
## ====================
## Variable: weight 
## 
##  Welch Two Sample t-test
## 
## data:  temp[[v]] by temp$asthma
## t = -0.35342, df = 327.66, p-value = 0.724
## alternative hypothesis: true difference in means between group 0 and group 1 is not equal to 0
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  -2.888426  2.008647
## sample estimates:
## mean in group 0 mean in group 1 
##        64.61944        65.05933 
## 
## 
## ====================
## Variable: bmi 
## 
##  Welch Two Sample t-test
## 
## data:  temp[[v]] by temp$asthma
## t = 0.32598, df = 342.42, p-value = 0.7446
## alternative hypothesis: true difference in means between group 0 and group 1 is not equal to 0
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  -0.6254995  0.8740164
## sample estimates:
## mean in group 0 mean in group 1 
##        23.37306        23.24880 
## 
## 
## ====================
## Variable: educ

##  Welch Two Sample t-test
## 
## data:  temp[[v]] by temp$asthma
## t = -3.2942, df = 320.19, p-value = 0.001098
## alternative hypothesis: true difference in means between group 0 and group 1 is not equal to 0
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  -0.5238182 -0.1320884
## sample estimates:
## mean in group 0 mean in group 1 
##        2.859864        3.187817 
## 
## 
## ====================
## Variable: fev1 
## 
##  Welch Two Sample t-test
## 
## data:  temp[[v]] by temp$asthma
## t = 0.22828, df = 342.09, p-value = 0.8196
## alternative hypothesis: true difference in means between group 0 and group 1 is not equal to 0
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  -91.49835 115.52584
## sample estimates:
## mean in group 0 mean in group 1 
##        3697.302        3685.288 
## 
## 
## ====================
## Variable: fvc 
## 
##  Welch Two Sample t-test
## 
## data:  temp[[v]] by temp$asthma
## t = -0.6578, df = 322.45, p-value = 0.5111
## alternative hypothesis: true difference in means between group 0 and group 1 is not equal to 0
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  -175.99537   87.79454
## sample estimates:
## mean in group 0 mean in group 1 
##        4251.737        4295.837 
##

## Variable: pm25

##  Welch Two Sample t-test
## 
## data:  temp[[v]] by temp$asthma

## alternative hypothesis: true difference in means between group 0 and group 1 is not equal to 0
## 95 percent confidence interval:

## sample estimates:
## mean in group 0 mean in group 1 
##        14.14327        14.37463 
##

## Variable: sulfate 
## 
##  Welch Two Sample t-test
## 
## data:  temp[[v]] by temp$asthma
## t = -1.3802, df = 373.13, p-value = 0.1684
## alternative hypothesis: true difference in means between group 0 and group 1 is not equal to 0
## 95 percent confidence interval:

## sample estimates:

## alternative hypothesis: true difference in means between group 0 and group 1 is not equal to 0
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  -0.024224139 -0.003591036
## sample estimates:
## mean in group 0 mean in group 1 
##       0.8138663       0.8277739 
## 
## 
## ====================
## Variable: dust 
## 
##  Welch Two Sample t-test
## 
## data:  temp[[v]] by temp$asthma
## t = -1.525, df = 370.37, p-value = 0.1281
## alternative hypothesis: true difference in means between group 0 and group 1 is not equal to 0
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  -0.08260296  0.01044360
## sample estimates:
## mean in group 0 mean in group 1 
##        1.104141        1.140221 
## 
## 
## ====================
## Variable: longitude 
## 
##  Welch Two Sample t-test
## 
## data:  temp[[v]] by temp$asthma
## t = -1.9062, df = 370.73, p-value = 0.05739
## alternative hypothesis: true difference in means between group 0 and group 1 is not equal to 0
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  -0.207304293  0.003220666
## sample estimates:
## mean in group 0 mean in group 1 
##       -118.0578       -117.9557 
##

## Variable: latitude 
## 
##  Welch Two Sample t-test
## 
## data:  temp[[v]] by temp$asthma
## t = 2.0313, df = 357.25, p-value = 0.04297
## alternative hypothesis: true difference in means between group 0 and group 1 is not equal to 0
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  0.0009916783 0.0613484488
## sample estimates:
## mean in group 0 mean in group 1 
##        34.11617        34.08500
## Checking for significant variables + getting results

results <- data.frame(
  variable = character(),
  p_value = numeric(),
  significant = character()
)

for (v in quant_vars) {
  temp <- na.omit(data[, c(v, "asthma")])
  test <- t.test(temp[[v]] ~ temp$asthma)
  
  results <- rbind(results, data.frame(
    variable = v,
    p_value = test$p.value,
    significant = ifelse(test$p.value < 0.05, "YES", "NO")
  ))
}
results 
##     variable     p_value significant
## 1         id 0.798218878          NO
## 2        age 0.280032372          NO
## 3     height 0.175023841          NO
## 4     weight 0.724000385          NO
## 5        bmi 0.744638799          NO
## 6       educ 0.001097607         YES
## 7       fev1 0.819562225          NO
## 8        fvc 0.511134212          NO
## 9       pm25 0.210882508          NO
## 10   sulfate 0.168359622          NO
## 11   nitrate 0.256717729          NO
## 12        ec 0.008384553         YES
## 13      dust 0.128119392          NO
## 14 longitude 0.057394826          NO
## 15  latitude 0.042969435         YES
## pulling significant variables

significant_vars <- results[results$p.value < 0.05, ]
significant_vars
## [1] variable    p_value     significant
## <0 rows> (or 0-length row.names)
##sorting by most significant
results[order(results$p_value), ]
##     variable     p_value significant
## 6       educ 0.001097607         YES
## 12        ec 0.008384553         YES
## 15  latitude 0.042969435         YES
## 14 longitude 0.057394826          NO
## 13      dust 0.128119392          NO
## 10   sulfate 0.168359622          NO
## 3     height 0.175023841          NO
## 9       pm25 0.210882508          NO
## 11   nitrate 0.256717729          NO
## 2        age 0.280032372          NO
## 8        fvc 0.511134212          NO
## 4     weight 0.724000385          NO
## 5        bmi 0.744638799          NO
## 1         id 0.798218878          NO
## 7       fev1 0.819562225          NO
results$decision <- ifelse(results$p_value < 0.05, "Significant", "Not Significant")
## bmi vs age histo / scatterplot

hist(data$bmi,
     col = rgb(1, 0, 0, 0.5),
     xlim = range(c(data$bmi, data$age)),
     main = "BMI vs Age Distribution",
     xlab = "Values")
## Red = BMI, Blue = Age

hist(data$age,
     col = rgb(0, 0, 1, 0.5),
     add = TRUE)

## Histogram was made to compare distributions, although BMI and Age are different scales

plot(data$age, data$bmi,
     main = "BMI vs Age",
     xlab = "Age",
     ylab = "BMI")
abline(lm(bmi ~ age, data = data), col = "blue")

## Trying Linear Regression w/ FEV Attempt 1

numeric_data <- data[, sapply(data, is.numeric)]

model <- lm(fev1 ~ ., data = numeric_data)
summary(model)
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = fev1 ~ ., data = numeric_data)
## 
## Residuals:
##      Min       1Q   Median       3Q      Max 
## -1316.63  -134.68    25.29   170.18   659.03 
## 
## Coefficients:
##                Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)  -9.765e+03  7.552e+03  -1.293   0.1964    
## id           -7.711e-04  4.977e-03  -0.155   0.8769    
## age           2.903e+01  1.431e+01   2.028   0.0428 *  
## male          3.395e+00  2.242e+01   0.151   0.8797    
## asthma       -3.708e+01  2.275e+01  -1.630   0.1036    
## height        4.934e+00  4.726e+00   1.044   0.2968    
## weight       -3.236e+00  5.776e+00  -0.560   0.5754    
## bmi           6.378e+00  1.629e+01   0.391   0.6955    
## educ         -7.058e+00  7.830e+00  -0.901   0.3677    
## BaseGasstove  4.470e+01  2.492e+01   1.793   0.0733 .  
## BasePets     -1.271e+01  1.768e+01  -0.719   0.4725    
## ETS_base     -1.312e+01  2.517e+01  -0.521   0.6022    
## wheeze       -3.171e+01  2.775e+01  -1.143   0.2535    
## fvc           7.441e-01  1.727e-02  43.085   <2e-16 ***
## pm25          1.624e+00  1.577e+01   0.103   0.9180    
## sulfate      -4.070e+02  1.828e+02  -2.226   0.0263 *  
## nitrate       3.111e+02  1.414e+02   2.200   0.0281 *  
## ec           -5.069e+02  3.201e+02  -1.583   0.1137    
## dust          2.647e+02  2.097e+02   1.262   0.2072    
## longitude    -4.705e+01  1.076e+02  -0.437   0.6620    
## latitude      1.024e+02  2.139e+02   0.479   0.6323    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 246 on 837 degrees of freedom
##   (142 observations deleted due to missingness)
## Multiple R-squared:  0.8779, Adjusted R-squared:  0.875 
## F-statistic: 300.9 on 20 and 837 DF,  p-value: < 2.2e-16
## Trying Linear Regression w/ FEV Attempt 2

numeric_only <- data[, sapply(data, function(x) {
  is.numeric(x) && length(unique(x)) > 2 
})]

model <- lm(fev1 ~ ., data = numeric_only)
summary(model)
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = fev1 ~ ., data = numeric_only)
## 
## Residuals:
##      Min       1Q   Median       3Q      Max 
## -1314.20  -135.11    24.53   171.12   659.63 
## 
## Coefficients:
##                Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)  -9.748e+03  7.546e+03  -1.292   0.1968    
## id           -7.729e-04  4.974e-03  -0.155   0.8765    
## age           2.884e+01  1.425e+01   2.024   0.0433 *  
## asthma       -3.693e+01  2.272e+01  -1.626   0.1044    
## height        4.986e+00  4.710e+00   1.059   0.2901    
## weight       -3.226e+00  5.773e+00  -0.559   0.5764    
## bmi           6.301e+00  1.627e+01   0.387   0.6987    
## educ         -7.165e+00  7.794e+00  -0.919   0.3582    
## BaseGasstove  4.463e+01  2.490e+01   1.792   0.0735 .  
## BasePets     -1.283e+01  1.765e+01  -0.727   0.4674    
## ETS_base     -1.307e+01  2.515e+01  -0.519   0.6036    
## wheeze       -3.194e+01  2.769e+01  -1.153   0.2491    
## fvc           7.450e-01  1.635e-02  45.574   <2e-16 ***
## pm25          1.555e+00  1.576e+01   0.099   0.9214    
## sulfate      -4.074e+02  1.827e+02  -2.230   0.0260 *  
## nitrate       3.115e+02  1.413e+02   2.205   0.0277 *  
## ec           -5.089e+02  3.197e+02  -1.592   0.1118    
## dust          2.652e+02  2.096e+02   1.265   0.2061

## latitude      1.018e+02  2.138e+02   0.476   0.6342    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 245.8 on 838 degrees of freedom
##   (142 observations deleted due to missingness)
## Multiple R-squared:  0.8779, Adjusted R-squared:  0.8751 
## F-statistic: 317.1 on 19 and 838 DF,  p-value: < 2.2e-16
names(numeric_only)
##  [1] "id"           "age"          "asthma"       "height"       "weight"      
##  [6] "bmi"          "educ"         "BaseGasstove" "BasePets"     "ETS_base"    
## [11] "wheeze"       "fev1"         "fvc"          "pm25"         "sulfate"     
## [16] "nitrate"      "ec"           "dust"         "longitude"    "latitude"
## Attempt 3 - FEV 

model  <- lm(fev1 ~ . - educ - id - BaseGasstove - BasePets - male - ETS_base - wheeze - asthma - townabbr - hisp - race, data = data)

summary(model)
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = fev1 ~ . - educ - id - BaseGasstove - BasePets - 
##     male - ETS_base - wheeze - asthma - townabbr - hisp - race, 
##     data = data)
## 
## Residuals:
##      Min       1Q   Median       3Q      Max 
## -1347.88  -138.81    22.84   166.85   692.19 
## 
## Coefficients:
##                               Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)                 -1.294e+04  7.474e+03  -1.731  0.08374 .  
## age                          2.952e+01  1.420e+01   2.079  0.03794 *  
## height                       5.484e+00  4.702e+00   1.166  0.24381    
## weight                      -3.798e+00  5.761e+00  -0.659  0.50987    
## bmi                          7.941e+00  1.625e+01   0.489  0.62524    
## HomeBuilt1980 or later      -2.806e+01  2.375e+01  -1.181  0.23783    
## HomeBuiltBefore 1960        -2.556e+01  2.234e+01  -1.144  0.25290    
## HomeBuiltUnknown or Missing  7.607e+00  2.836e+01   0.268  0.78858    
## fvc                          7.439e-01  1.640e-02  45.359  < 2e-16 ***
## pm25                         1.432e+00  1.567e+01   0.091  0.92724    
## sulfate                     -4.437e+02  1.821e+02  -2.437  0.01503 *  
## nitrate                      3.641e+02  1.395e+02   2.609  0.00923 ** 
## ec                          -6.568e+02  3.196e+02  -2.055  0.04016 *  
## dust                         3.382e+02  2.093e+02   1.616  0.10646    
## longitude                   -7.262e+01  1.078e+02  -0.673  0.50090    
## latitude                     1.024e+02  2.140e+02   0.478  0.63242    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 246.6 on 842 degrees of freedom
##   (142 observations deleted due to missingness)
## Multiple R-squared:  0.8765, Adjusted R-squared:  0.8743 
## F-statistic: 398.4 on 15 and 842 DF,  p-value: < 2.2e-16
## Scatterplot w/ regression line ~ FEV1

ggplot(data = data, aes(x = age, y = fev1)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE) +
  labs(title = "FEV1 vs Age",
       x = "Age",
       y = "FEV1")
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'
## Warning: Removed 17 rows containing non-finite outside the scale range
## (`stat_smooth()`).
## Warning: Removed 17 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range
## (`geom_point()`).

ggplot(data = data, aes(x = male, y = fev1)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE) +
  labs(title = "FEV1 vs Male(sex)",
       x = "Male",
       y = "FEV1")
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'
## Warning: Removed 17 rows containing non-finite outside the scale range
## (`stat_smooth()`).
## Removed 17 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range
## (`geom_point()`).

ggplot(data = data, aes(x = bmi, y = fev1)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE) +
  labs(title = "FEV1 vs BMI",
       x = "BMI",
       y = "FEV1")
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'
## Warning: Removed 17 rows containing non-finite outside the scale range
## (`stat_smooth()`).
## Removed 17 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range
## (`geom_point()`).

## Each plot compares FEV1 to a continuous variable (age, sex, bmi) + removed 17 rows containing non-finite outside scale range and missing values/ values outside scale
## Scatterplot w/ regression line ~ FVC

ggplot(data = data, aes(x = age, y = fvc)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE) +
  labs(title = "FEV1 vs Age",
       x = "Age",
       y = "FVC")
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'
## Warning: Removed 4 rows containing non-finite outside the scale range
## (`stat_smooth()`).
## Warning: Removed 4 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range
## (`geom_point()`).

ggplot(data = data, aes(x = male, y = fvc)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE) +
  labs(title = "FEV1 vs Male(sex)",
       x = "Male",
       y = "FVC")
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'
## Warning: Removed 4 rows containing non-finite outside the scale range (`stat_smooth()`).
## Removed 4 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range
## (`geom_point()`).

ggplot(data = data, aes(x = bmi, y = fvc)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE) +
  labs(title = "FEV1 vs BMI",
       x = "BMI",
       y = "FVC")
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'
## Warning: Removed 4 rows containing non-finite outside the scale range (`stat_smooth()`).
## Removed 4 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range
## (`geom_point()`).

## Each plot compares FVC to a continuous variable (age, sex, bmi) + removed 4 rows containing non-finite outside scale range and missing values/ values outside scale
##Trying out Logistic Regression FEV vs Asthma

table(data$asthma)
## 
##   0   1 
## 787 209
model <- glm(asthma ~ fev1, data = data, family = binomial)
summary(model)
## 
## Call:
## glm(formula = asthma ~ fev1, family = binomial, data = data)
## 
## Coefficients:
##               Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)   
## (Intercept) -1.218e+00  4.232e-01  -2.878    0.004 **
## fev1        -2.497e-05  1.128e-04  -0.221    0.825   
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## (Dispersion parameter for binomial family taken to be 1)
## 
##     Null deviance: 1012.7  on 978  degrees of freedom
## Residual deviance: 1012.6  on 977  degrees of freedom
##   (21 observations deleted due to missingness)
## AIC: 1016.6
## 
## Number of Fisher Scoring iterations: 4
exp(coef(model))
## (Intercept)        fev1 
##   0.2958272   0.9999750
exp(confint(model))
## Waiting for profiling to be done...
##                 2.5 %    97.5 %
## (Intercept) 0.1288714 0.6781466
## fev1        0.9997521 1.0001947
clean_data <- na.omit(data)

model <- glm(asthma ~ fev1,
             data = clean_data,
             family= binomial)

clean_data$predicted_prob <- predict(model, type = "response")

#plotting logistic curve
library(ggplot2)

ggplot(data, aes(x = fev1, y = asthma)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_smooth(method = "glm", method.args = list(family = "binomial")) +
  labs(title = "Logistic Regression: Asthma vs FEV1")
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'
## Warning: Removed 21 rows containing non-finite outside the scale range
## (`stat_smooth()`).
## Warning: Removed 21 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range
## (`geom_point()`).

## removed 21 rows containing non-finite outside scale range and missing values/ values outside scale
